# Arena

> Arena is dependency injection for **test infrastructure**. It gives each ExUnit
> test its own isolated copy of the process-based infrastructure your app shares
> in production — the Ecto sandbox connection, a `Phoenix.PubSub` server, the
> process Registry namespace, application-env values — so process-heavy apps run
> `async: true` with zero crosstalk. The same production code routes to per-test
> instances through a process-dictionary config (`via_tuple()`), with **no code
> changes between prod and test**.

The problem Arena solves: spawned processes (GenServers, Tasks, supervised
children, LiveView/Channel processes) do **not** inherit a test's sandbox
transaction or per-test infrastructure. With Arena you build a per-test
`Arena.Config`, `Arena.wrap/2` it into child specs, and `use Arena.Process` /
`use Arena.Supervisor` so each process unwraps the config, stores it in its
process dictionary, and runs integration callbacks (e.g. `Ecto.Sandbox.allow`)
at `init`. Client calls use `via_tuple()`, which reads the config and routes to
the per-test instance in tests, or the global one in prod.

## Read in this order

- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): the mental model — the three pieces (Config / Wrapped /
  Process), how config flows down the process tree, the core patterns and
  anti-patterns, and a quick reference. **Start here.**
- [README.md](README.md): the full feature guide — installation, setup, every
  usage pattern, integrations, advanced features, troubleshooting.
- [docs/testing-phoenix.md](docs/testing-phoenix.md): **the non-obvious part** —
  how a per-test config reaches the *connected* LiveView / Channel process, why
  naive PubSub isolation silently loses messages, and the on_mount/connect_info
  recipe. Read this before isolating PubSub in a Phoenix app.
- [docs/integrations-roadmap.md](docs/integrations-roadmap.md): existing
  integrations and concrete proposals for new ones (Mox bridge, Phoenix
  on_mount helpers, Credo checks, a base case template, …).

## Critical facts an agent MUST know (each one causes SILENT failures if missed)

- **Wrap every spawn.** `start_supervised!({Server, Arena.wrap(config, args)})`,
  never `{Server, args}`. An unwrapped child starts under the shared
  `:arena_global` owner and silently cross-talks — no error is raised. Same for
  `DynamicSupervisor.start_child` and supervisor children.
- **`Arena.Config.store/1` must run before any spawn.** Store-after-spawn is the
  most common silent-crosstalk cause; the test process itself needs the config
  stored so its own `via_tuple()` calls resolve to the per-test instance.
- **Arena breaks at a real HTTP/transport boundary.** Process-dictionary config
  cannot cross a process that doesn't inherit the dictionary (a real Bandit/
  Cowboy worker, a browser WebSocket in Wallaby/Playwright). For those, fall back
  to `Application.put_env` + `async: false`, or thread state via the
  `Phoenix.Ecto.SQL.Sandbox` user-agent-metadata pattern. **But** `Phoenix.ConnTest`,
  `Phoenix.LiveViewTest`, and `Phoenix.ChannelTest` all run **in-process** (no real
  transport), so they ARE Arena-eligible and run `async: true` — see
  [docs/testing-phoenix.md](docs/testing-phoenix.md).
- **Arena and Mox are complementary, not alternatives.** Use Arena for internal
  process collaborators (start a real, isolated copy); use Mox for the external
  HTTP-service boundary (a behaviour contract). Prefer Arena over mocking an
  internal GenServer.
- **A connected LiveView/Channel process is reachable.** It runs under the ExUnit
  test supervisor and has the **test pid in its `$callers`** (not `$ancestors`);
  its `connect_info` in tests is the pruned `Plug.Conn` (with `conn.private`
  intact). An `on_mount` hook can therefore store the test's config in it — this
  is exactly how the Ecto sandbox grants the connected LV DB access. See the
  Phoenix doc.
- **PubSub isolation is all-or-nothing per test.** A per-test PubSub server only
  isolates if *every* broadcaster AND subscriber in the test resolves the *same*
  per-test server. One broadcaster that resolves the global server (an unwrapped
  process, or a missed call site) silently drops the message. Resolve the server
  name from `Arena.Config` everywhere, with a `$callers` fallback.

## Core API

- `Arena.setup/1,2` → build a per-test `Arena.Config` from the ExUnit context
  (unique owner from `{module, test}`).
- `Arena.wrap/2` → `%Arena.Wrapped{config, input}` envelope for a child spec.
- `Arena.Config`: `new/1,2`, `store/1`, `current/0`, `get/1,2`, `put/2,3`,
  `add_callback/1,2`, `execute_callbacks/1`, `child/2`. Read context with
  `get/2`; it raises on absent keys, so for optional keys read
  `Arena.Config.current().context` with `Map.fetch/2`.
- `use Arena.Process` (GenServers): injects `start_link/1`, `via_tuple/0`,
  `to_process_key/1` callback, `get_pid/1`, `alive?/1`. Override `to_process_key/1`
  (`__MODULE__` for singletons; `{__MODULE__, id}` for keyed). Client calls use
  `via_tuple()`, never `__MODULE__`. Keep `handle_*` callbacks free of Arena.
- `use Arena.Supervisor` (Supervisors): wrap children manually with
  `Arena.wrap(Arena.Config.current(), args)` in `init`.
- `Arena.Task.async/1,2`, `Arena.Task.async_stream/3` → tasks that carry the
  config (DB access, etc.), replacing `Task.async`.

## Integrations

- `Arena.Integrations.Ecto.setup(config, repo: Repo)` → adds the callback that
  `Ecto.Adapters.SQL.Sandbox.allow`s each wrapped child onto the test's
  connection. First thing to check on `DBConnection.OwnershipError`.
- `Arena.Integrations.PubSub.setup(config, opts)` → starts a per-test
  `Phoenix.PubSub` server (`start_supervised!`) and stores its name at the
  `:pubsub_name` context key. Resolve the server from config in your
  broadcast/subscribe calls.
- `ArenaApplication.get_env/3` (+ `fetch_env/2`, `fetch_env!/2`, `put_env/3,4`,
  `merge_env/3,4`) → async-safe drop-in for `Application.get_env/3`. Reads a
  per-test override from `Arena.Config` (namespaced by `{app, key}`), else the
  application env. Replaces `Application.put_env`-in-test (and the `async: false`
  it forces). `merge_env` does a surgical partial override of a map/keyword value.

## Optional

- [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md): release notes.
- `test/arena/*_test.exs`: executable usage examples for every module/integration.
- Diagnostics: `ArenaInspector.assert_arena_configured!()` and an `ArenaDebugger`
  (`has_arena_config?/1`, `check_process_registration/1`) — port into your
  `test/support` to debug "no process" / crosstalk issues.
